reasoning: The base rate for communication between Trump and Zelenskyy is currently very high. They have effectively been speaking every few weeks during early 2025 as active peace negotiations and ceasefires are deliberated. Confirmed recent calls occurred on February 12, March 19, May 8, and a joint call on May 10. The resolution window spans 34 days (May 12 to June 15), meaning a typical 2-4 week frequency of direct calls makes a conversation highly likely. Furthermore, a 30-day ceasefire was proposed on May 8, which points to an explicit follow-up being necessary around June 7 (comfortably inside the window). The G7 summit is scheduled for June 15-17, meaning any preparatory discussions will also fall exactly in the May 12-June 15 timeframe. Both leaders have a consistent track record of publicly acknowledging their calls, satisfying the resolution criteria. The main risks to a YES resolution are a sudden severed relationship where they refuse to speak directly (which hasn't happened despite past 'heated' clashes) or if all intermediate talks are delegated to envoys. However, given Trump's preference for direct leader-to-leader diplomacy in this conflict, the probability of them speaking on the phone is extremely high.
